page: Microsoft Azure DC64as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AMD EPYC 7763 processor, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, though it is premium storage capable and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations are highly efficient, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA extensions, resulting in strong Passmark encryption scores. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is strong, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier. Additionally, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in text generation for small LLM models, alongside strong database operations and Redis SET throughput.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DC64as_cc_v5 offers high compute density and dedicated resource allocation, making it a viable option for enterprise workloads requiring predictable performance without the overhead of bare-metal hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on external premium storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy tasks. This server is highly suited for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, cryptography tasks, web serving, and small-scale language model inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC64as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, this server features 32 physical cores with hyper-threading and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core CPU performance, excellent cryptographic execution, and highly efficient database operations, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. While uncached memory bandwidth and single-core processing remain average, its dedicated resource allocation provides highly predictable performance. This configuration is well-suited for enterprise database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, cryptographic workloads, and general-purpose web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC64as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, ideal for databases and cryptographic workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC64as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, optimized for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 64-vCPU AMD EPYC platform with 256 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for enterprise databases and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 64-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz.
Equipped with 256 GB of system memory (4 GB per core) and no local storage.
Strong multi-core CPU, database operations, and cryptographic benchmark performance.
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale language model inference.
Best suited for enterprise databases, multi-threaded data processing, and web serving.
